howItWorks.intro: This emergency generator load calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Running Load = Essential Loads + Motor Loads
Starting Load = Essential Loads + (Motor Load x Starting Multiplier)
Generator kVA = Peak Load / Power Factor / 1000
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: Why does motor starting load matter for generator sizing?
faq.answer: Electric motors draw 3-6x their running current when starting. If the generator cannot handle this surge, it will stall or trip breakers. Always size for the largest motor starting simultaneously with other loads.

faq.question: What power factor should I use?
faq.answer: Use 0.8 for mixed residential loads. Industrial motor loads may be 0.7-0.85. Resistive loads (heaters, lights) are 1.0. Lower power factor means you need a larger generator.

howWeCalculate.intro: This emergency generator load calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
